meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement analytics without coding experience. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive SaaS growth.
content.answer_block: You don't need to be an engineer to track user behavior. By leveraging Antigravity for visual integration and Claude Code for automated implementation, you can deploy professional-grade analytics in minutes. This stack bypasses complex coding, allowing you to focus on conversion metrics and data-driven product decisions immediately.
content.intro:
The silent killer of most SaaS startups isn't a bad idea; it's the 'blind spot.' You launch your MVP, push traffic to your landing page, and then stare at a blank dashboard wondering why the conversion rate is flat. Without analytics, you are flying a plane in the dark without instruments, waiting for a crash that could have been avoided.

But the technical barrier has long intimidated non-technical founders. You don't need a degree in computer science to understand your users. By combining the visual orchestration of Antigravity with the autonomous coding power of Claude Code, you can wire up your app’s heartbeat in a single afternoon. Let's turn your guesswork into a growth engine.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that define success (e.g., Sign-up, Add-to-Cart, Subscription). This prevents data bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Growth Engineer. Create a tracking schema for a SaaS MVP focusing on user acquisition and conversion events. Define the event names and properties needed for PostHog.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my tracking schema and generate the necessary environment variables for my project.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate your chosen analytics SDK (e.g., PostHog or Mixpanel) into your project structure without manually editing files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable analytics utility file in TypeScript that initializes PostHog using environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create analytics.ts and update the main application entry point to initialize the provider.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Actions
steps.description: Wrap your UI buttons and form submissions with tracking events to measure actual user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a helper function 'trackEvent(name, properties)' that can be called from any React component to send data to the analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the SignupForm component to trigger a trackEvent call on successful submission.'

steps.title: Automate Data Validation
steps.description: Ensure your events are actually firing correctly by running automated tests against your implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that validates if the analytics provider is initialized and if an event is triggered when the test simulation runs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file to verify analytics event emission and run it to ensure no errors.'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Push your changes to production and verify the data flow in your analytics dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a deployment checklist for a founder to verify analytics data flow from a production environment to the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'What are the best practices for monitoring production analytics for silent failures?'

pro_tips: Always track 'abandonment' events—knowing where users drop off is more valuable than knowing where they sign up.
Use distinct user IDs from day one; anonymous sessions are useful, but identified sessions drive revenue strategies.
Keep your event taxonomy simple: 'Action_Object' (e.g., Click_PricingBtn) is the gold standard for clean reporting.
Don't over-track; prioritize high-value behavioral data to avoid 'analysis paralysis' and high storage costs.
Set up an automated alert in your analytics tool for when conversion rates drop by more than 10% in a 24-hour window.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 7 days gathering baseline data. Use that data to run an A/B test on your primary CTA and iterate based on the results.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start scaling. With your analytics pipeline automated, you now have the clarity to make high-stakes product decisions with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
